prose:
Method–provenance block
Property: Atom-condensed Fukui functions (f+, f−) and dual descriptors for phenol; most electrophilic site identified by f+ maximum.
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier ‘standard’), no density fitting, run in PySCF 2.5.0 via chemkit.
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (phenol.xyz); single-point evaluation – no re-optimization.
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model).
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet).  N±1 states treated as doublets (cation and anion, charge ±1, multiplicity 2) per default.
Convergence & warnings: SCF cycles for all three charge states converged; integrity.status = ok, integrity.trustworthy = true.  No warnings reported by the engine.
Live log: the run streamed to a live .out file during execution (path printed at launch in the caller’s working directory; retain for full SCF and fukui details).  Result JSON written to phenol_fukui_dft.json.

Headline result
The most electrophilic atom (largest f+) is C3 (index 2, counting from 0) with f+ = 2.1028319.  The most nucleophilic atom (largest f−) is O1 (index 0) with f− = 0.16201477.

Per-atom condensed Fukui indices (parent atom order):
 Atom  f+          f−           Dual descriptor
  O1   −0.99513677  0.16201477 −1.1571515
  C2    0.39933189  0.048772551 0.35055934
  C3    2.1028319   0.070972379 2.0318595 ← most electrophilic
  C4    1.6196806   0.070498539 1.5491820
  C5   −0.34200645  0.026704972 −0.36871143
  C6   −0.28910827  0.020949414 −0.31005769
  C7    1.3079753   0.12351549  1.1844598
  H8   −0.73391778  0.082321145 −0.81623893
  H9   −0.27465615  0.088147788 −0.36280394
  H10  −0.91706058  0.081105985 −0.99816657
  H11  −0.62991625  0.080487046 −0.71040330
  H12  −0.29591676  0.093495770 −0.38941253
  H13   0.047899333 0.051014152 −0.0031148187

The calculation employed the chemkit ‘fukui-reactivity’ skill, which computes atom-condensed Fukui functions by finite-difference of Mulliken charges over neutral, cationic (N−1), and anionic (N+1) single-point DFT energies.

All data were obtained computationally; provenance: computed.
